name: Dioxus
description: A fullstack, cross-platform Rust framework for building web, desktop, and mobile apps from a single codebase

description.value: A fullstack, cross-platform Rust framework for building web, desktop, and mobile apps from a single codebase

active-maintenance.comment: Very active development with full-time team, regular releases, hot-patching feature added in v0.7

reactivity-model.comment: Signal-based reactivity with automatic dependency tracking; uses Sledgehammer for optimized DOM operations

component-model.comment: Components defined as Rust functions with #[component] attribute; React-like functional component model

rendering-paradigm.comment: Declarative RSX syntax similar to JSX; uses Virtual DOM with efficient diffing via Sledgehammer

state-management-approach.comment: Built-in signals with use_signal, use_context for dependency injection, and GlobalSignal for app-wide state

opinionatedness.comment: Provides integrated fullstack capabilities with recommended patterns but allows flexibility in architecture

backward-compatibility.comment: Still pre-1.0 (v0.7.x); APIs are stabilizing but breaking changes occur between versions

compiles-to.comment: Compiles to WebAssembly for web; native binaries for desktop and mobile

typescript-support.comment: Rust framework; not applicable - uses Rust's type system instead

template-syntax.comment: rsx! macro with JSX-like syntax for defining views; HTML elements and Rust expressions combined

styling-approach.comment: No built-in styling; built-in Tailwind CSS support via dx CLI; community crates like dioxus_style for scoped CSS

build-required.comment: Requires Rust toolchain and dx CLI for development; handles building, bundling, and hot-reloading

wasm-interop.comment: Uses wasm-bindgen and web-sys for JavaScript interop; can call into native code (JNI, Objective-C, web-sys)

reactive-primitives.comment: use_signal creates reactive state; Signals are Copy and auto-track dependencies

computed-values.comment: Derived values via closures that read signals; signals automatically track dependencies

two-way-binding.comment: oninput and value binding for form elements; signals enable reactive two-way patterns

event-handling.comment: onclick, oninput, onsubmit and other event attributes in rsx! macro

conditional-rendering.comment: Standard Rust if/else and match expressions in rsx! macro

list-rendering.comment: Iterator methods (.iter(), .map()) with rsx! macro for efficient list rendering

component-slots.comment: children prop for content projection; Element type for slot-like patterns

teleport-portal.comment: document::* elements for rendering to document head; Portal support for out-of-tree rendering

suspense.comment: SuspenseBoundary component for async loading boundaries; integrates with SSR streaming

error-boundaries.comment: ErrorBoundary component for catching and handling errors in component tree

context-provide-inject.comment: use_context_provider and use_context for dependency injection without prop drilling

refs-direct-dom-access.comment: onmounted callback and web-sys integration for direct DOM manipulation

official-router.comment: dioxus-router is the official first-party routing solution, included in main dioxus crate

nested-routes.comment: Full support via #[nest] attribute and Outlet component; deeply nested layouts supported

dynamic-routes.comment: Parameterized routes with typed parameters in route enum variants

route-guards.comment: Navigation guards via redirect mechanisms and conditional routing

lazy-loading-routes.comment: WASM splitting with wasm-split feature; requires --experimental-wasm-split flag

scroll-restoration.comment: Built-in scroll restoration support in router

built-in-state.comment: use_signal for local reactive state with automatic dependency tracking

built-in-global-state.comment: GlobalSignal for app-wide state without boilerplate; also Context API with use_context

global-state-library.comment: Built-in GlobalSignal and Context API sufficient; no external state library needed

devtools-state-inspection.comment: Community dioxus-devtools available but no official browser extension yet

state-persistence.comment: No built-in persistence layer; available via community crates

time-travel-debugging.comment: Not officially supported; signal-based reactivity focus on forward state changes

official-cli.comment: dx CLI for project scaffolding, building, bundling, and hot-reloading; inspired by wasm-pack and webpack

hot-module-replacement.comment: Subsecond hot-patching of Rust code at runtime (v0.7+); preserves app state during edits

browser-devtools.comment: Community dioxus-devtools available; no official browser extension

vs-code-extension.comment: Official Dioxus VS Code extension from DioxusLabs for workspace configuration and tooling

error-messages.comment: Rust's excellent compiler errors plus Dioxus-specific diagnostics; dioxus-check for static analysis

documentation-quality.comment: Comprehensive docs with guides, API reference on docs.rs; docs built with Dioxus itself and run CI

playground.comment: Dioxus Playground exists at docsite but requires server compilation; not a traditional REPL

bundle-size-core.comment: ~50 KB WASM gzipped for minimal Hello World app; significantly smaller than initial 1-2MB

bundle-size-full.comment: ~70 KB WASM gzipped with optimizations; roughly on par with react-dom. WASM parses faster than equivalent JS.

virtual-dom.comment: Uses Virtual DOM with optimized diffing via Sledgehammer; bump allocator for efficiency

compile-time-optimization.comment: Rust compiler performs extensive optimizations; rsx! macro generates efficient code at compile time

tree-shaking.comment: Rust's dead code elimination; wasm-opt and dx bundle further optimize WASM binaries

code-splitting.comment: WASM splitting via wasm-split feature for route-based code splitting

lazy-components.comment: Lazy loading with wasm-split and SuspenseBoundary for on-demand component loading

ssr-capable.comment: Full SSR support with hydration via dioxus-fullstack; progressive opt-in SSR

streaming-ssr.comment: HTML streaming with out-of-order streaming via enable_out_of_order_streaming()

partial-hydration.comment: Progressive hydration supported; components rerun on client to reconstruct state

js-framework-benchmark.comment: Estimated ~1.10; uses Sledgehammer (3rd fastest framework); beats React, Vue, on par with Leptos and SolidJS

npm-weekly-downloads.comment: Rust crate, not npm package; distributed via crates.io

component-libraries.comment: dioxus-tw-components, dioxus-material, community components; smaller ecosystem than Leptos

meta-framework.comment: Dioxus itself is fullstack; no separate meta-framework needed. Integrates deeply with Axum.

major-companies-using.comment: Airbus and ESA building collision avoidance system; Huawei shipping production apps; Satellite.im building Discord competitor

official-sponsor-backer.comment: Supported by FutureWei, Satellite.im, and GitHub Accelerator program; full-time team of engineers

discord-community-size.comment: ~6,172 members in Dioxus Labs Discord as of December 2025

stack-overflow-questions.comment: Very limited Stack Overflow presence; community primarily uses Discord and GitHub Discussions

job-market-demand.comment: Niche; primarily adopted by Rust enthusiasts and teams already using Rust for cross-platform apps

official-testing-library.comment: No official testing library; relies on Rust's built-in testing with wasm-bindgen-test

testing-library-name.comment: Recommended approach: Rust's built-in #[test] with wasm-bindgen-test for WASM testing

component-testing.comment: Via Rust's built-in testing framework; ui-dioxus-test crate available for component testing

e2e-testing-integration.comment: Works with standard E2E tools; Playwright/Cypress can test Dioxus web apps

snapshot-testing.comment: Supported via Rust's insta crate for snapshot testing

mobile-framework.comment: Built-in mobile support via dioxus-mobile; dx serve --platform android/ios for native mobile apps

pwa-support.comment: Can be configured as PWA; compatible with service worker patterns

desktop-framework.comment: Built-in desktop support via dioxus-desktop; uses Tauri WebView for <3MB portable binaries

learning-curve.comment: Steep learning curve; requires Rust knowledge plus React-like concepts (signals, hooks, RSX)

concepts-to-learn.comment: Rust ownership, signals, hooks, effects, RSX syntax, server functions, routing, context, fullstack patterns

time-to-hello-world.comment: ~5 minutes with Rust toolchain installed; dx new my-app creates working project immediately

migration-path.comment: No migration guides from JS frameworks; fundamentally different paradigm (Rust vs JavaScript)

incremental-adoption.comment: Cannot be incrementally added to existing JS applications; requires full Rust toolchain
